body_txt: How the characters represent these roles varies. The "Combat" position is most clear by being the best of the three in direct combat, though how they fight may vary, with them engaging with melee weaponry, firearms, their bare hands or even shapeshifting to a combat-ready form. While the "Magic" position is usually a magic user, despite the title they may use powers more fitting of the setting such as Psychic Powers or Ki Manipulation , or enter Magic from Technology territory, being a Gadgeteer Genius or a master hacker in a technological environment. They may not be the only member of the group with their respective power/tech, but they will have the highest affinity with it. The "Trickery" position is the loosest of the three, with the most sure thing about them is that they have abilities tied to their craftiness. They will likely take the role of The Sneaky Guy , being better at finding things and remaining unfound than their two allies and/or The Face , taking care of the trio's social situations. If they are a combatant, expect them to use a less direct combat style than their Combat counterpart, using sneak attacks, Hit-and-Run Tactics , or Confusion Fu . Note to qualify, the three characters must consistently fit into their roles. If two (or all three) members of the trio switch roles depending on the situation, then the Combat, Magic, Trickery dynamic does not apply. In a Freudian Trio , typically the hard-hitting, battle-oriented Id is linked to Combat and a knowledgeable, superstitious Superego is linked to Magic, leaving the tricky, self-serving Ego linked to Trickery. Of course this isn't always the case . This trope can sometimes crossover with Beauty, Brains, and Brawn , if the Beauty uses their good looks to their social advantage and the Brain's powers come from knowledge and intellect. The Brawn meanwhile will have the physical strength to prove themselves as quite the fighter. Compare with Strength, Sorcery, Finesse , when these positions are three gameplay options rather than three specific characters, although these two tropes can intersect. Also compare with similar Power Trio tropes Physical, Mystical, Technological and Speed, Smarts and Strength ; as well as Action Hero , Science Hero , and Guile Hero . As there is no evidence of magic nor super science in the real world, there is no known person that could qualify for the "Magic" role and therefore, No Real Life Examples, Please!
